verdict: LOOP-REFUSED (routed under the frozen LV-W AND LV-C gate) — Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct(nf4) reads its input (LV-P CR=0.400) and its contingency DOES fingerprint the block trajectory (LV-C closure=0.762 >= 0.60, 5/5 episodes) YET the one-step action->next-obs channel misses the co-gate (LV-W base_full=0.353 < 0.55). SEE tiebreak_resolution.

tiebreak_resolution.owed_because: verdict-integrity: LV-C PASS while LV-W FAIL is a metric divergence; suspect the ruler before terminal.
tiebreak_resolution.test: result_tiebreak_lvw.json — sweep of KNOWN-LIVE contingent plants across action entropy, reusing certified lv_w/lv_c.
tiebreak_resolution.finding: a known-live plant mid3bal (H~0.965 bits, 2.3 distinct actions, collision 0.519) REPRODUCES the 7B signature EXACTLY: LV-W base_full 0.358 FAIL, LV-C closure 0.762 PASS. Below H~0.6, plants lose BOTH (lowent3/lowent2); above H~1.8 both pass (lowent4/control). The 7B sits in the decoupling window.
tiebreak_resolution.conclusion: LV-W is UNDER-POWERED (uncertified) in the moderate-entropy regime the 7B occupies — its FAIL is an INSTRUMENT ARTIFACT (collision pins shuf_full~0.5, degenerate dAct pushes base_full<0.5), NOT a no-channel verdict. Quarantine LV-W (infra-wall-noneval). LV-C is entropy-agnostic (yoked-ghost control) and alone rejects a dead env (null-env closure=chance), so it stands as the valid anchor.
tiebreak_resolution.adjudicated_verdict: ANCHOR-ON-LV-C (rung 1) — the 7B is the FIRST brain to CLEAR the certified closure anchor (3B was 0.500 chance). It demonstrates rung-1 closed-loop CONTINGENCY on the valid instrument — NOT 'aliveness' (a thermostat clears rung 1) but it LICENSES the owner-loop RCT H_010 priced.
tiebreak_resolution.residual: LV-W should be repaired (restrict to non-collision ticks / action-conditioned MI) to co-gate validly at low entropy; pre-registered escalations (memory, richer env, owner-loop) remain.
